engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz E Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz E Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The OM651 became the backbone of Mercedes diesel sales across the W212 E-Class, prized for fuel economy but notorious for timing chain issues in higher-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: E220 CDI, E220 BlueTEC, E220 CDI BlueEFF, E250 CDI, E250 BlueTEC, E250 CDI 4MATIC, E300 Hybrid, E200 CDI
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure, typically after 60,000 miles
Injector seal leakage causing diesel creep and combustion gas blow-by
Oil cooler seal degradation leading to external oil leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes introduced the all-aluminium OM654 to replace the OM651, bringing stepped bowl combustion and reduced friction. Early units suffered teething problems with AdBlue systems.
commonFailures: AdBlue system malfunctions including tank crystallisation and NOx sensor failure
Timing chain stretch on higher-mileage units
Injector seal failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Diesel
entries.history: The OM648 straight-six was the last of Mercedes' inline-six diesels before the V6 era, prized for smoothness and durability. It remains a favourite among W211 enthusiasts.
commonFailures: Injector seal leakage causing diesel contamination of engine oil
Crankshaft position sensor failure leading to hot-start issues
EGR valve clogging from soot accumulation
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM648 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M274 turbocharged four-cylinder brought modern efficiency to the E-Class petrol range, but gained an unfortunate reputation for catastrophic piston and cylinder failures.
commonFailures: Piston cracking and cylinder wall scoring, sometimes requiring full engine replacement
Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure causing rough running
Low-speed pre-ignition (LSPI) damage in higher mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M274 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: The M276 naturally aspirated V6 delivered refined power for the E350 petrol, but its direct injection system introduced carbon build-up issues not seen in port-injected predecessors.
commonFailures: Intake valve carbon build-up requiring walnut blasting
Camshaft adjuster failure
Oil consumption from worn piston rings
entries.cta: Get quotes for M276 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The supercharged M271 brought forced induction to the E-Class entry-level petrol range, but a poorly designed camshaft drive system created chronic timing issues.
commonFailures: Camshaft adjuster (cam gear) failure from worn internal locking pins
Timing chain stretch and guide breakage, often by 80,000 miles
Balancer shaft sprocket wear on early pre-facelift units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 6.2L V8 Petrol
entries.history: The M156 was Mercedes-AMG's first independently developed V8, producing a naturally aspirated 514hp. It remains a legend among enthusiasts, despite well-documented mechanical weaknesses.
commonFailures: Head bolt failure causing coolant leaks into cylinders, requiring immediate replacement
Camshaft adjuster wear and lifters failing (bucket tappet tick)
Intake manifold flap linkage failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M156 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L V8 Petrol
entries.history: The M177 hot-vee twin-turbo V8 replaced the M156 in the E63 S, delivering massive power with cylinder deactivation for cruising efficiency.
commonFailures: Cylinder bore scoring, particularly in higher-mileage examples
Turbocharger oil feed line leaks
High-pressure fuel pump failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for M177 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

entries.title: 5.0L V8 Petrol
entries.history: The M113 V8 was a robust, chain-driven unit that preceded the M156 in the E500, known for durability but ageing electronics.
commonFailures: ECU failure (Bosch ME2.8) causing running issues and fault codes
Camshaft position sensor failure
Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets
entries.cta: Get quotes for M113 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz E Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Benz E Class OM651 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz E Class OM651 Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.1 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz E Class E220 CDI, E220 BlueTEC, E250 CDI (2009-2016, OM651.924)
problems.rootCause: The simplex timing chain sits at the rear of the engine, making replacement a gearbox-out job. Chain stretch occurs when oil change intervals exceed 15,000 miles, allowing guide wear and tensioner fatigue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of timing chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles; only viable if caught before chain jumps

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain set plus replacement of damaged valves, lifters, and camshaft adjusters
repairOptions.longevity: 40,000-60,000 miles; requires head removal and specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM651 with upgraded timing components and bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; upgraded parts address original design weakness

problems.recommendation: On an E220 CDI worth £2,500-£5,000, preventative chain replacement at a specialist is the clear value play if you hear cold-start rattle. If the chain has already jumped and bent valves, a rebuilt engine often costs less than a dealer-led valvetrain rebuild - and you get a fresh bottom end with warranty.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 Mercedes Benz E Class engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz E Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz E Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure - The Costly V6 Diesel Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz E Class E320 CDI, E350 CDI, E350 BlueTEC, E350d (2005-2023, OM642.850/858)
problems.rootCause: The internal oil cooler sits in the engine Vee and uses rubber seals that harden with heat cycles, causing oil to leak into the Vee, run down the block, and eventually mix with coolant.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Labour to remove intake manifold, replace cooler seals, clean oil residue, refill fluids
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles; no upgraded parts but fixes the leak

repairOptions.tier: Major - Oil Cooler Seals Plus Ancillaries
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Seal replacement plus new turbo oil feed lines, injector seals, and intake gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; addresses multiple OM642 leak points

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM642 with upgraded Viton oil cooler seals and new turbo lines
repairOptions.longevity: 140,000+ miles; upgraded seals solve the original design flaw permanently

problems.recommendation: If caught early with no oil-coolant emulsion, specialist seal replacement is all you need. Once oil has blocked the turbo feed lines or emulsified coolant has damaged bearings, a rebuilt engine with upgraded seals becomes the most cost-effective long-term solution - especially on a high-value E350 BlueTEC.
problems.cta: Get OM642 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement quotes

problems.group: Mercedes Benz E Class M274 Piston Crack
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz E Class M274 Piston Crack - The 2.0 Petrol Failure That Writes Off Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz E Class E200, E250, E300 (2013-2023, M274)
problems.rootCause: Low-speed pre-ignition (LSPI) combined with oil dilution causes detonation events that crack pistons between ring lands, typically on cylinder two or three.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned short block fitted with your existing ancillaries
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000-100,000 miles; cranks and pistons new, head reused

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt M274 with forged pistons and upgraded rings
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; stronger pistons prevent repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of low-mileage used M274 from salvage
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-80,000 miles; no upgrade - same design flaw remains

problems.recommendation: There is no minor repair for a cracked piston - the engine must come apart. On a 2.0 petrol E-Class worth £6,000-£12,000, a specialist-supplied rebuilt engine with forged pistons costs marginally more than a used replacement but eliminates the repeat failure risk. Avoid dealer pricing here - the premium buys no design improvement.
problems.cta: Compare M274 Mercedes Benz E Class rebuilt engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz E Class M271 Camshaft Adjuster Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz E Class M271 Camshaft Adjuster Failure - The Kompressor Achilles Heel
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz E Class E200 Kompressor, E200 (2002-2009, M271)
problems.rootCause: The camshaft adjuster (cam gear) uses spring-loaded locking pins that wear, causing rattle on startup and eventual loss of timing accuracy, bending valves.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of both cam gears, timing chain, and tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles; works if engine still runs and valves are intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cam gears, chain, plus replacement of bent valves and lifters
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-80,000 miles; head removal and specialist reassembly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M271 with revised cam adjuster design
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; upgraded adjusters solve original failure cause

problems.recommendation: On an older W211 E200 Kompressor worth £1,500-£3,000, replacement often exceeds the car's value. A specialist rebuild or low-mileage used engine is the pragmatic choice. Do not wait until the rattle becomes a misfire - cam adjuster failure always bends valves eventually.
problems.cta: Get M271 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement quotes

problems.group: Mercedes Benz E Class M156 Head Bolt Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz E Class M156 Head Bolt Failure - The 6.2 V8 AMG Time Bomb
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz E Class E63 AMG (W211, 2006-2011, M156)
problems.rootCause: Early M156 engines used low-spec head bolts with aluminium threads that corrode and snap, allowing coolant to enter combustion chambers - hydraulic locking follows.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of all head bolts with updated steel items, head gaskets, and coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Full engine life; must be done pre-failure - no symptoms

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt M156 with upgraded head bolts, cam adjusters, and lifters
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles; comprehensive rebuild addresses all AMG weak points

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of used M156 from salvage
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown history; original head bolt design remains a risk

problems.recommendation: If you own a W211 E63 AMG that has NOT yet suffered head bolt failure, preventative replacement at an AMG specialist is essential - it is the single best value modification you can make. Once coolant has entered a cylinder, engine replacement is the only safe outcome. Never buy a used M156 without documented head bolt upgrade.
problems.cta: Compare M156 AMG Mercedes Benz E Class engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz E Class
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your E Class diesel is suffering timing chain rattle, your V6 is weeping oil from the cooler seals, or your AMG is due preventative head bolts, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz E Class specialists. Every rebuilt engine in our network includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices, choose a specialist, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz E Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz E Class across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest diesel option in the E Class range appeared in the W212 generation as an economy-focused entry point for fleet buyers. The OM626 1.6 CDI offered genuine 60+ mpg but was rarely specified on UK executive saloons, making replacement units less common than larger diesels.
commonFailurePoints: DPF blockage from low-speed urban driving
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of E Class diesel sales across W212 and late W211 generations, the 2.1 CDI in OM651 and OM646 forms powered thousands of UK taxis and company cars. Known for excellent economy but let down by timing chain vulnerabilities, this displacement remains the most frequently replaced diesel unit in the range.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The OM647 2.7 CDI served as a mid-range diesel option in earlier W211 and W210 E Class models, sitting between the 2.1 and 3.2 units. While less common on UK roads today, these engines are known for robust bottom ends but age-related injector and turbo issues.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The OM642 V6 turbodiesel defined E Class refinement across three generations, delivering effortless torque for motorway cruising. Despite its popularity in E320 CDI, E350 CDI and E350 BlueTEC models, the infamous oil cooler seal failure makes this displacement one of the most common specialist replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 3.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The OM648 straight-six diesel from the W211 and late W210 generations is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most durable diesel engines. Its smoothness and reliability make it a favourite among enthusiast owners, though injector seal failures become common beyond 100,000 miles.
commonFailurePoints: Injector seal leakage causing combustion blow-by
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class 3.2L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M271 1.8 Kompressor engine brought supercharged performance to the entry-level E Class petrol range during the W211 and early W212 generations. Its mechanical supercharger provided eager response, but camshaft adjuster failures have made these engines a common replacement candidate in older examples.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M274 turbocharged four-cylinder replaced the M271 in the W212 facelift and carried through to the W213, offering modern efficiency and torque. However, widespread piston cracking issues have made this displacement a frequent replacement enquiry, particularly in E200 and E300 models.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder bore scoring from low-speed pre-ignition
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M276 3.5 V6 naturally aspirated engine offered a smooth, refined petrol alternative to diesel in the W212 E350. Less common than its diesel counterpart, these units suffer from carbon build-up on intake valves due to direct injection, though catastrophic failures are relatively rare.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M113 5.0 V8 powered the E500 in the W211 generation, delivering relaxed V8 performance for executive buyers who avoided diesel. These chain-driven V8s are generally robust, but ageing electronics and oil leaks send some units to specialist rebuilders.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M156 6.2 V8 in the W211 E63 AMG remains a naturally aspirated icon, producing 514hp without turbochargers. However, its reputation is undermined by head bolt failures that can destroy engines catastrophically - making preventative replacement or rebuilt units essential for serious owners.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class 6.2L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The W213 E300e combined the M274 2.0 petrol engine with a plug-in hybrid system, offering company car tax benefits alongside electric-only city driving. Replacement enquiries typically involve the combustion engine's standard M274 failures rather than hybrid-specific components.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz E Class 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M177 4.0 V8 biturbo in the W213 E63 S delivers devastating performance with cylinder deactivation for cruising. Replacement costs are exceptionally high, with cylinder bore scoring and turbo oil feed issues being the primary failure drivers for this hot-vee design.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz E Class 4.0L petrol engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz E Class needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz E Class specialists.

fuelTypes.tag: Mercedes Benz E Class Engines Fuel Type
fuelTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz E Class Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mercedes Benz E Class engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early W210 diesel workhorses to the latest W213 plug-in hybrids.

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominated E Class sales across W211, W212 and W213 generations, with the OM651 2.1 and OM642 3.0 V6 accounting for over 70% of UK replacement enquiries due to timing chain and oil cooler failures.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominated E Class sales across W211, W212 and W213 generations, with the OM651 2.1 and OM642 3.0 V6 accounting for over 70% of UK replacement enquiries due to timing chain and oil cooler failures.
families: OM651 - 2.1 litre four-cylinder, timing chain stretch epidemic in E220 CDI/E250 CDI
OM642 - 3.0 litre V6, oil cooler seal failure on E320 CDI/E350 BlueTEC/E350d
OM654 - 2.0 litre four-cylinder, AdBlue system faults in W213 E220d
OM648 - 3.2 litre straight-six, injector seal leaks in W211 E320 CDI
OM647 - 2.7 litre five-cylinder, turbo vane sticking in W210/W211 E270 CDI
OM611 - 2.2 litre four-cylinder, injector issues in early W210 E220 CDI
knownFor: Strong motorway economy and high-mileage durability
OM651 timing chain failures requiring gearbox-out replacement
OM642 oil cooler seals leaking into the engine Vee from 70k miles
AdBlue tank crystallisation and NOx sensor faults on BlueTEC models
typicalModels: E320 CDI, E350 CDI, E350 BlueTEC, E350d
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol E Class variants generate lower overall replacement volume than diesel but attract higher average order value, particularly for V6 M276 units and AMG models where owners demand specialist-grade rebuilds.
items.descriptor: Petrol E Class variants generate lower overall replacement volume than diesel but attract higher average order value, particularly for V6 M276 units and AMG models where owners demand specialist-grade rebuilds.
families: M274 - 2.0 litre turbo, piston cracking in W212/W213 E200/E250/E300
M276 - 3.5 litre V6, carbon build-up on intake valves in W212 E350
M271 - 1.8 litre supercharged, camshaft adjuster failure in W211/W212 E200 Kompressor
M156 - 6.2 litre V8, head bolt corrosion in W211 E63 AMG
M177 - 4.0 litre biturbo V8, cylinder bore scoring in W213 E63 S
M113 - 5.0 litre V8, ECU failure in W211 E500
knownFor: Refined, high-revving character compared to diesel
M274 piston failure typically between 50k-90k miles with no warning
M156 head bolt failure can hydrolock engine without preventative work
Lower replacement volume but higher rebuild standards expected
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class Petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The W213 E300e combined the M274 2.0 petrol engine with an electric motor and battery pack, offering low company car tax rates and electric-only city driving.
items.descriptor: The W213 E300e combined the M274 2.0 petrol engine with an electric motor and battery pack, offering low company car tax rates and electric-only city driving.
families: M274 (PHEV variant) - 2.0 litre turbo with electric drive module
OM654 (PHEV variant) - E300de diesel plug-in hybrid (limited UK volume)
Electric motor integrated between engine and automatic gearbox
13.5kWh battery offers up to 30 miles of electric-only range
Petrol engine takes over automatically for longer journeys
importantNotes: Petrol engine suffers same M274 piston cracking as non-hybrid variants
High-voltage battery inspection recommended during engine replacement
Specialist hybrid-trained fitters required for safe powertrain removal
Replacement enquiries often driven by engine failures, not hybrid components
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz E Class Plug-in Hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes Benz E Class Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Later W213 and W214 E Class models introduced 48-volt mild hybrid systems on petrol and diesel engines, providing torque assist and coasting functionality without electric-only driving.
items.descriptor: Later W213 and W214 E Class models introduced 48-volt mild hybrid systems on petrol and diesel engines, providing torque assist and coasting functionality without electric-only driving.
families: M256 - 3.0 litre straight-six petrol with integrated starter-generator (ISG)
OM654 (MHEV) - 2.0 litre diesel with belt-driven starter-generator (BSG)
OM656 (MHEV) - 3.0 litre straight-six diesel with ISG
48-volt battery powers a starter-generator replacing the alternator
Provides up to 20hp electric boost during acceleration
Enables engine-off coasting to reduce fuel consumption
importantNotes: MHEV component failure is less common than on full hybrids or BEVs
48-volt battery replacement is separate from engine replacement cost
Standard engine rebuilds still apply - hybrid system does not alter core failure points
Availability through specialists is good as MHEV becomes standard on newer models
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz E Class Mild Hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz E Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers every major Mercedes Benz E Class engine change from 1995 to the present, helping owners identify their exact power unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The W210 introduced the E-Class name officially, replacing the 124 series, and brought multi-link rear suspension and improved aerodynamics.
keyChanges: First generation officially branded as E-Class (replacing 124 series)
Introduced OM606 diesel and M112/M113 V6/V8 petrol engines
4MATIC all-wheel drive available on select variants
Facelift in 1999 with updated lighting and interior
knownFor: Rust issues on front wings and rear arches
OM606 diesel known as one of Mercedes' most robust engines
E55 AMG supercharged V8 a performance icon
Electronic throttle and suspension gremlins with age
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-2002 Mercedes Benz E Class replacement engines
years.ticker: OM606 injector pump timing issues • E220 CDI OM611 injector failure • W210 rust-related engine bay corrosion

years.description: The W211 became a UK taxi and fleet favourite, with the OM642 V6 diesel arriving in 2005 and the M156 6.2 V8 powering the first E63 AMG.
keyChanges: Introduced SBC (Sensotronic Brake Control) on early models
OM642 V6 diesel debuted in 2005
E63 AMG replaced E55 AMG with naturally aspirated M156 V8 (2006)
2006 facelift removed SBC and updated front end
mainEngines: OM642 - 3.0 litre V6 diesel (E320 CDI facelift/E350 CDI/E350 BlueTEC)
knownFor: OM642 oil cooler seal failure - very common from 70k miles
M156 head bolt corrosion - replace pre-emptively on early cars
SBC brake pump failures on pre-facelift (now extended warranty expired)
W211 remains a high-mileage workhorse with strong resale
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2009 Mercedes Benz E Class W211 replacement engines
years.ticker: OM642 oil cooler seal repair cost • E220 CDI timing chain failure • M156 head bolt replacement • E63 AMG engine replacement price
enquiries: OM642 oil cooler seal repair cost

years.description: The OM651 2.1 diesel arrived with the W212, quickly becoming the UK's most common E-Class engine for private hire and fleet operators.
keyChanges: New chassis with sharper, more angular styling
OM651 2.1 diesel introduced - became the UK volume leader
2013 facelift with updated headlamps and new engine options
M274 2.0 turbo petrol replaced M271
E63 AMG switched from M156 to M157 5.5 biturbo V8 (2012-2013), then M177 4.0 V8 (2014-2016)
mainEngines: OM651 - 2.1 litre diesel (E200 CDI/E220 CDI/E250 CDI)
OM642 - 3.0 litre V6 diesel (E350 CDI/E350 BlueTEC)
knownFor: OM651 timing chain stretch - major replacement driver
AdBlue system failures on BlueTEC models
M274 piston cracking - known issue from 2013 onwards
Improved rust resistance over W211
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2016 Mercedes Benz E Class W212 replacement engines
years.ticker: OM651 timing chain replacement cost • E220 CDI engine seized • M274 piston crack • AdBlue tank failure E350 BlueTEC

years.description: The W213 brought mild hybrid technology, the new OM654 diesel, and the most powerful E63 yet with the M177 engine.
keyChanges: New platform with improved aerodynamics and tech
OM654 2.0 diesel replaced OM651 (E220d)
First E-Class PHEV - E300e (M274 petrol with electric motor)
M256 3.0 straight-six mild hybrid petrol (E450)
2020 facelift with updated lights and MBUX
knownFor: OM654 AdBlue system faults
M274 piston issues continued into early W213
M177 cylinder bore scoring concerns
Strong resale values despite complex electronics
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2023 Mercedes Benz E Class W213 replacement engines
years.ticker: E220d AdBlue engine start limit • M274 cracked piston E300 • E63 S M177 engine replacement cost
enquiries: E63 S M177 engine replacement cost

years.description: The latest E-Class introduces more powerful mild hybrid systems across the range and refined four-cylinder diesels with 48-volt technology.
keyChanges: All-new platform with hybrid standard on most engines
OM654 mild hybrid diesel with belt-driven starter-generator (BSG)
OM656 straight-six diesel for E450d
M254 2.0 petrol with integrated starter-generator (ISG)
M256 3.0 straight-six mild hybrid petrol
mainEngines: OM654 MHEV - 2.0 litre mild hybrid diesel (E220d)
OM656 MHEV - 3.0 litre straight-six mild hybrid diesel (E450d)
M254 - 2.0 litre mild hybrid petrol (E200/E300e PHEV)
knownFor: M254/M256 mild hybrid reliability still unproven at high mileage
OM656 oil cooler seal concerns (carried over from OM642 design)
Complex electronics require specialist diagnostic equipment
New generation means fewer high-mileage replacement examples yet
years.cta: Get quotes for 2023 onwards Mercedes Benz E Class W214 replacement engines

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz E Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Mercedes E-Class?
items.answer: Yes, if the car is otherwise sound and the replacement cost is under £4,000 supply and fit. E-Classes hold value better than most executive cars, making replacement viable even at 120,000+ miles.
keyPoints: A quality rebuilt OM651 or OM642 costs £1,500-£3,500 supply only
W211/W212 examples with full service history still command £3,000-£7,000 used
A replacement engine with warranty costs less than financing a newer car
Specialist fitting adds £500-£800, not dealer rates of £1,500+
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mercedes Benz E Class engine prices for your mileage and budget

items.question: What is the most reliable Mercedes E-Class engine to buy used?
items.answer: The OM648 3.2 straight-six diesel in the W211 (2002-2005) is widely regarded as bulletproof. The later OM654 2.0 diesel in post-2017 W213s is also proving very solid with proper maintenance.
keyPoints: OM648 injector seals fail but the bottom end is near-indestructible
OM654 avoids the timing chain issues of the OM651 completely
M276 3.5 petrol V6 is the most reliable petrol option
Avoid early OM651 (2009-2012) unless timing chain already done
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes Benz E Class engine with warranty for your chosen variant

items.question: What are the common problems with the Mercedes E-Class OM642 V6 diesel?
items.answer: The OM642's primary failure is oil cooler seal degradation - a £5 part requiring 8-10 hours of labour. EGR valves, PCV valves, and timing chain stretch also occur but less frequently.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seals fail around 70k-100k miles; cost £700-£1,100 at a specialist
Look for oil drips under the engine bay - that's the telltale sign
Front main bearing failure can occur if oil filters aren't pre-filled
Later 2014+ OM642s have improved seals but can still fail
items.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How much does a Mercedes E-Class engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines range from £1,200 for OM651 units to £5,000+ for OM656 six-cylinders. With specialist fitting, budget £1,800-£4,500 total for most diesel variants.
keyPoints: OM651 2.1 diesel: £1,200-£2,800 supply only
OM642 3.0 V6 diesel: £1,500-£3,500 supply only
OM654 2.0 diesel (2016+): £1,800-£3,800 supply only
M156 6.2 V8 AMG: £4,500-£9,000 supply only
Labour adds £400-£800 depending on specialist location
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement costs by entering your reg now

items.question: What causes the OM651 timing chain to fail on E220 CDI models?
items.answer: The simplex timing chain is mounted at the rear of the engine and stretches when oil changes exceed 15,000 miles. Extended service intervals are the primary culprit.
keyPoints: Chain stretch typically occurs between 60k-100k miles
Cold-start rattle is the first warning sign - don't ignore it
Gearbox-out job costs £1,100-£1,600 at a specialist
If the chain jumps, bent valves require a full rebuild or replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz E Class timing chain replacement before it's too late

items.question: Which Mercedes E-Class year has the best and worst engines?
items.answer: Best: 2002-2005 W211 with OM648, or 2017+ W213 with OM654. Worst: 2009-2012 W212 with early OM651 timing chain issues and AdBlue teething problems.
keyPoints: 2006-2008 OM642s had the worst oil cooler seal failure rates
2013-2016 M274 petrols suffer piston cracking
2017 onward OM654 diesels are significantly improved
W213 E220d owners report 60+ mpg and £20 road tax
items.cta: Find a Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement for your specific model year

items.question: Is the Mercedes E-Class E300e plug-in hybrid engine reliable?
items.answer: The M274 petrol engine in the E300e suffers the same piston cracking issues as non-hybrid versions. The hybrid system itself is generally reliable, but two complex systems mean more potential failure points.
keyPoints: Piston failure typically occurs between 50k-90k miles
High-voltage battery inspection is essential during engine replacement
Hybrid variants are often £1k-£2k cheaper used - for good reason
Only specialist hybrid-trained fitters should work on these systems
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes Benz E Class hybrid engine availability from qualified specialists

items.question: Should I buy a petrol or diesel Mercedes E-Class in 2026 considering ULEZ?
items.answer: Both are ULEZ-compliant from 2015 onwards. Diesel still makes sense for high-mileage motorway drivers; petrol suits lower annual mileage and urban use. Choose based on driving pattern, not just emissions.
keyPoints: Euro 6 diesels (2015+) meet ULEZ and CAZ requirements
E220d returns 55-60 mpg real world vs 35-40 mpg for E200 petrol
Petrol E200 avoids AdBlue system failures common on BlueTEC diesels
Diesel OM654 tax can be as low as £20/year on post-2017 models
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz E Class petrol or diesel engine replacement based on your usage

items.question: How long does a Mercedes E-Class engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance and 8,000-10,000 mile oil changes, most E-Class diesel engines reach 200,000-250,000 miles. OM648 and OM654 examples regularly exceed 300,000 miles in taxi and fleet use.
keyPoints: OM648 straight-six is known for 300k+ miles on original internals
OM651 can reach 200k+ with timing chain replaced once
OM654 in taxis has proven reliable beyond 150k miles
Short oil change intervals (8k miles) are critical for timing chain life
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz E Class engine replacement quotes - be ready when the time comes

items.question: Can I replace my E220d engine with an E350d engine?
items.answer: Physically yes, but it's rarely cost-effective. The OM654 2.0 and OM642 3.0 V6 use different wiring looms, ECUs, gearboxes, and cooling systems. Stick with the correct engine code for your chassis.
keyPoints: Engine mounts and physical fitment may work, but electronics don't match
DVLA notification and insurance approval required for any swap
Specialist fabrication costs usually exceed selling the car and buying the desired variant
The 9G-Tronic gearbox is mapped specifically to each engine
items.cta: Find the correct Mercedes Benz E Class engine for your exact chassis and engine code
